enrichment.short_description: Sprout Social is a cloud-based social media management platform that helps businesses schedule content, manage a unified inbox, run social listening, and report on performance across major social networks.
enrichment.seo_title: Sprout Social Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Plans
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sprout Social pricing starts at $79/seat/month. Compare plans, features, pros and cons of this social media management and analytics platform.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Sprout Social is a publicly traded (NASDAQ: SPT) social media management platform founded in 2010 and headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. It helps marketing, customer care, and sales teams schedule and publish content, manage a unified social inbox, run social listening and competitive analysis, and generate performance reports across networks including Instagram, Facebook, X, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, and Pinterest. It is built for small businesses through large enterprises that manage multiple social profiles, collaborate across teams, and need to prove social media ROI. Pricing is subscription-based and billed per seat: Essentials starts at 79 dollars per seat per month (billed annually, or 99 dollars billed monthly) for up to 5 profiles, Standard is 199 dollars per seat per month, Professional is 299 dollars per seat per month with unlimited profiles, and Advanced is 399 dollars per seat per month with AI-enhanced replies, sentiment analysis, and API access. An Enterprise tier offers custom pricing with white-glove onboarding and dedicated support. All paid plans include a 30-day free trial with no credit card required. Add-on modules for premium analytics, social listening, and influencer marketing and employee advocacy are sold separately from the core plans. Sprout Social competes with Hootsuite, Buffer, and Later, and is best known for its deep analytics, unified inbox, and enterprise-grade reporting.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Sprout Social
paragraphs: Sprout Social is a social media management and analytics platform used by marketing, customer care, and sales teams to plan, publish, monitor, and report on social media activity from a single dashboard.

Founded in 2010 in Chicago, the company went public on the Nasdaq in 2019 and has grown into one of the most widely used enterprise-grade social media suites, serving agencies, mid-market brands, and Fortune 500 companies alongside smaller businesses.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: At its core, Sprout Social combines a content calendar and publishing queue with a unified social inbox that consolidates messages, comments, and mentions from every connected profile into one stream.

Higher tiers add social listening, sentiment analysis, competitor benchmarking, review management, employee advocacy, and an influencer marketing module, along with customizable reporting dashboards that can be exported or shared with stakeholders.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Sprout Social prices per seat across five tiers: Essentials (79 dollars/month annual), Standard (199 dollars/month), Professional (299 dollars/month), Advanced (399 dollars/month), and a custom-priced Enterprise tier.

Nearly all plans require annual prepayment, though a monthly option exists at a premium, and every tier comes with a 30-day free trial. Premium analytics, social listening, and influencer/employee advocacy tools are separate paid add-ons not bundled into the base plans.

features.title: Publishing and Scheduling
features.description: Plan and queue posts across Instagram, Facebook, X, LinkedIn, TikTok, Pinterest, and YouTube with a visual content calendar and optimal send-time suggestions.

features.title: Unified Smart Inbox
features.description: Consolidates comments, DMs, and mentions from every connected profile into one stream so teams can respond without switching tabs.

features.title: Social Listening
features.description: Tracks keywords, hashtags, and brand mentions across the social web to surface trends, sentiment, and competitive intelligence.

features.title: Analytics and Reporting
features.description: Customizable, exportable reports covering engagement, audience growth, paid performance, and competitor benchmarks.

features.title: Review Management
features.description: Monitors and responds to reviews on Google, Facebook, and other review sites from within the same dashboard.

features.title: AI Assist
features.description: Generates and refines post copy, suggests reply language, and summarizes sentiment using built-in AI tools.

features.title: Employee Advocacy and Influencer Marketing
features.description: Add-on modules that let brands amplify content through employee networks and manage influencer partnerships and payouts.

features.title: Approval Workflows and Team Collaboration
features.description: Content approval chains, task assignment, and message tagging for cross-functional marketing and support teams.

pros: Deep, exportable analytics and enterprise-grade reporting
Genuinely unified inbox across all major networks
Strong, responsive customer support
Scales well from small teams to large enterprises
Social listening add-on gives real competitive insight
cons: Expensive compared to lighter competitors like Buffer
Most plans require annual prepayment
Best features (listening, API access, advanced automation) are locked behind top tiers or paid add-ons
Per-seat pricing gets costly fast for larger teams
Interface has a learning curve for teams new to the platform

faqs.question: How much does Sprout Social cost
faqs.answer: Plans start at 79 dollars per seat per month billed annually (Essentials) and go up to 399 dollars per seat per month (Advanced), with a custom-priced Enterprise tier above that.

faqs.question: Does Sprout Social have a free trial
faqs.answer: Yes, most plans include a 30-day free trial that does not require a credit card.

faqs.question: Which social networks does Sprout Social support
faqs.answer: Instagram, Facebook, X, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, Pinterest, and Google Business Profile, among others depending on the plan.

faqs.question: Is Sprout Social good for agencies
faqs.answer: Yes, agencies commonly use the Professional or Advanced plans for unlimited profiles, client reporting, and approval workflows.

faqs.question: Does Sprout Social include social listening
faqs.answer: Basic listening features are included on some plans, but full social listening is a separate paid add-on module.

faqs.question: Can I cancel Sprout Social anytime
faqs.answer: Annual plans are prepaid for the year; monthly billing is available on some tiers at a higher rate for more flexibility.

faqs.question: What is Sprout Social's parent company
faqs.answer: Sprout Social, Inc. is an independent public company (Nasdaq: SPT) founded in 2010, not owned by another SaaS holding company.

faqs.question: How does Sprout Social compare to Hootsuite
faqs.answer: Sprout Social is generally considered stronger in analytics, reporting, and customer support, while Hootsuite offers a wider range of lower-priced plans.

use_cases.title: Enterprise social customer care
use_cases.description: Support teams use the unified inbox and approval workflows to manage high volumes of customer messages across networks with consistent response times.
use_cases.audience: Enterprise customer support and social care teams

use_cases.title: Multi-brand agency management
use_cases.description: Agencies use unlimited profiles, white-label reporting, and content tagging to manage many client accounts from one login.
use_cases.audience: Digital marketing and social media agencies

use_cases.title: Brand growth and competitive analysis
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use social listening and competitor benchmarking to track sentiment, campaign performance, and share of voice.

enrichment.short_description: Spruce Health is a HIPAA-compliant healthcare communication platform that unifies secure messaging, telemedicine video visits, business phone, and e-fax for clinics and care teams.
enrichment.seo_title: Spruce Health Pricing and Features Review 2026
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spruce Health pricing starts at $24/user/month. Compare its HIPAA-compliant messaging, telehealth, and e-fax plans, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Spruce Health is a HIPAA-compliant healthcare communication platform founded in 2013 and headquartered in San Francisco, California. It centralizes patient-facing communication for clinics, private practices, and care teams into one interface, combining secure two-way texting, HIPAA-compliant video telemedicine visits, a shared team inbox, business VoIP phone lines, e-fax, and automated appointment reminders. It is designed for solo practitioners, group practices, and healthcare organizations that need to replace disconnected phone systems, personal cell phones, and fax machines with a single compliant tool. Pricing is per user, per month, on month-to-month billing with no long-term contract required: the Basic plan is 24 dollars per user per month and covers phone calls, secure messaging, video telemedicine, e-fax, and team chat; the Communicator plan is 49 dollars per user per month and adds phone trees, time-of-day call routing, VoIP desk phones, API access, and bulk broadcast messaging. A 14-day free trial is available without a credit card. Spruce Health is commonly compared to tools like Weave, OhMD, and Klara in the patient communication and virtual care space, and it is best known for combining fax, phone, video, and messaging into a single HIPAA-compliant inbox rather than requiring separate point solutions.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Spruce Health
paragraphs: Spruce Health is a healthcare communication platform that gives clinics and independent practices one HIPAA-compliant inbox for phone calls, secure texting, video visits, e-fax, and team chat.

Founded in 2013 and based in San Francisco, Spruce Health was built to replace the mix of personal cell phones, fax machines, and unsecured texting many small healthcare practices rely on with a single compliant system.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform's core is a shared team inbox where staff can send secure messages, place and receive calls, send e-faxes, and conduct video telemedicine visits, with conversations assignable to specific teammates or groups.

Automated tools include appointment reminders, intake questionnaires, scheduled messages, and bulk broadcast messaging, while the Communicator plan adds business-grade phone infrastructure like auto-attendants, call routing rules, and VoIP desk phone support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Spruce Health charges per user per month with two plans: Basic at 24 dollars per user per month and Communicator at 49 dollars per user per month for practices needing more advanced phone and workflow customization.
Billing is month-to-month with no long-term contract, and a 14-day free trial is available without requiring a credit card.

features.title: HIPAA-Compliant Secure Messaging
features.description: Two-way SMS-style texting with patients that stays compliant, including file sharing and read receipts.

features.title: Video Telemedicine
features.description: Secure, HIPAA-compliant video visits that patients can join from a browser link with no app download required.

features.title: Business Phone and E-Fax
features.description: A dedicated business phone number with calling, voicemail transcription, and e-fax sending and receiving from any device.

features.title: Shared Team Inbox
features.description: Conversations can be assigned, tagged, and paged to specific teammates or groups so nothing falls through the cracks.

features.title: Automated Patient Communication
features.description: Scheduled messages, appointment reminders, and intake questionnaires reduce no-shows and manual outreach.

features.title: Mobile Payments and Billing Requests
features.description: Send payment requests to patients directly through the messaging thread on select plans.

features.title: Call Routing and Phone Trees
features.description: On the Communicator plan, practices can build auto-attendant phone trees and route calls based on time of day.

features.title: API Access
features.description: The Communicator plan exposes an API for integrating Spruce communication data with EHRs and other practice systems.

pros: Combines phone, fax, text, and video into one compliant inbox
Month-to-month billing with no long-term contract
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Simple, transparent per-user pricing
Well suited to small practices replacing multiple disconnected tools
cons: Advanced phone routing and API access require the pricier Communicator plan
Limited native EHR integrations compared to some larger competitors
Per-user pricing can add up for practices with many staff accounts
Some advanced workflow automation may require add-ons or higher plans
Smaller company size than some enterprise-focused competitors

faqs.question: How much does Spruce Health cost
faqs.answer: The Basic plan is 24 dollars per user per month and the Communicator plan is 49 dollars per user per month, both billed month-to-month.

faqs.question: Is Spruce Health HIPAA compliant
faqs.answer: Yes, Spruce Health is built specifically for HIPAA-compliant communication including messaging, calls, and video visits with patients.

faqs.question: Does Spruce Health offer a free trial
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available and does not require a credit card.

faqs.question: Can Spruce Health replace my clinic's fax machine
faqs.answer: Yes, e-fax sending and receiving from any device is included on both pricing plans.

faqs.question: Does Spruce Health support video telemedicine visits
faqs.answer: Yes, secure HIPAA-compliant video visits are included starting on the Basic plan.

faqs.question: Is there a long-term contract with Spruce Health
faqs.answer: No, Spruce Health uses month-to-month billing by default and customers can cancel at any time.

faqs.question: Who is Spruce Health best suited for
faqs.answer: Solo practitioners, small group practices, and healthcare teams that want to consolidate phone, text, fax, and video into one compliant tool.

faqs.question: Does Spruce Health integrate with EHR systems
faqs.answer: API access is available on the Communicator plan for integrating with other practice systems, though native EHR integrations are more limited than some competitors.

use_cases.title: Solo and small group medical practices
use_cases.description: Replace personal cell phones and unsecured texting with a single compliant inbox for patient communication.

use_cases.title: Telehealth-first care teams
use_cases.description: Run entire patient visits through secure video calls combined with messaging and intake forms.

use_cases.title: Multi-provider group practices
use_cases.description: Route calls, assign conversations, and manage shared patient communication across a larger clinical staff.

features: Phone trees and time-of-day call routing

enrichment.short_description: SpyFu is a competitor keyword research tool that reveals the SEO and PPC keywords, ad history, and backlinks that competitors use to rank and run paid search campaigns.
enrichment.seo_title: SpyFu Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SpyFu pricing starts at $29/month. See competitor keyword research, PPC, and backlink features, plans, pros and cons of this SEO tool.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SpyFu is a competitor research tool for SEO and pay-per-click marketing, founded in 2005 and headquartered in Scottsdale, Arizona. It is built for freelancers, in-house marketers, and agencies who want to see which keywords a competitor ranks for organically and bids on in paid search, along with their historical ad copy, backlinks, and ranking history going back more than a decade. Its signature Kombat tool visualizes keyword overlap between up to three domains using a Venn-diagram style comparison to surface gaps and opportunities. SpyFu is privately held and has a small team, commonly cited around 11 to 50 employees. Pricing is subscription-based with no permanently free plan: the Basic plan starts at 29 dollars per month billed annually (39 dollars billed monthly) for a single site and limited exports; the Pro plus AI plan is 89 dollars per month billed annually (119 dollars monthly) and adds unlimited exports, ten years of historical data, and AI features including RivalFlow and ChatGPT integration; the Team or Agency plan is 187 dollars per month billed annually (249 dollars monthly) with five user seats, white-label reporting, and API access. All plans come with a 30-day money-back guarantee. SpyFu is frequently compared to Ahrefs, SEMrush, and Moz, and is generally positioned as a lower-cost alternative focused specifically on competitor intelligence rather than full-suite SEO auditing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SpyFu
paragraphs: SpyFu is a competitor intelligence tool for SEO and PPC marketers that shows which keywords a domain ranks for organically, which keywords it bids on in Google Ads, and how its ad copy and rankings have changed over time.

Founded in 2005 and based in Scottsdale, Arizona, SpyFu built its reputation on historical search and ad data, giving users the ability to look back over a decade of a competitor's SEO and paid search activity.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The core workflow starts by entering a competitor's domain to pull organic keyword rankings, estimated traffic, PPC keywords, ad history, and backlinks, all searchable and filterable by position or search volume.

The Kombat tool compares up to three domains at once to visualize keyword overlap and gaps, while Backlink Kombat surfaces sites linking to competitors but not to you, and RivalFlow uses AI to recommend content and keyword opportunities based on competitor gaps.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SpyFu offers three tiers: Basic at 29 dollars per month (annual billing), Pro plus AI at 89 dollars per month (annual billing), and Team or Agency at 187 dollars per month (annual billing), each roughly 25 percent higher when billed monthly instead of annually.
There is no permanently free plan, but every tier is covered by a 30-day money-back guarantee, and higher tiers unlock more historical data, tracked keywords, exports, and user seats.

features.title: Competitor Keyword Research
features.description: See every organic keyword a domain ranks for in the top 100 results, including search volume, position, and estimated clicks.

features.title: PPC Ad History
features.description: View a competitor's historic Google Ads copy and the keywords they have bid on going back more than a decade.

features.title: Kombat Keyword Overlap
features.description: Compares up to three domains in a Venn-diagram style view to find shared and missed keyword opportunities.

features.title: Backlink Analysis and Backlink Kombat
features.description: Identify a competitor's backlink profile and find sites linking to rivals but not to your own domain.

features.title: Rank Tracking
features.description: Track weekly keyword ranking changes for your own site and monitor competitors over time.

features.title: RivalFlow AI
features.description: AI-powered recommendations that flag content and keyword gaps based on competitor performance.

features.title: Automated Reporting
features.description: Scheduled, white-labelable SEO and PPC reports for clients or stakeholders on higher-tier plans.

features.title: Domain and Keyword Exports
features.description: Bulk data exports of keyword and competitor data for further analysis outside the platform.

pros: Significantly cheaper than Ahrefs or SEMrush for competitor research
Deep historical PPC ad data going back over a decade
Kombat tool makes keyword gap analysis fast and visual
30-day money-back guarantee on every plan
Simple, focused interface centered on competitor intelligence
cons: No permanently free plan
Keyword database and site audit depth are lighter than full-suite tools like Ahrefs
Basic plan limits exports and tracked keywords significantly
Additional user seats cost extra even on the Team plan
Less robust technical SEO auditing compared to dedicated crawlers

faqs.question: How much does SpyFu cost
faqs.answer: Plans start at 29 dollars per month billed annually (Basic) up to 187 dollars per month billed annually for the Team or Agency plan.

faqs.question: Does SpyFu have a free trial or free plan
faqs.answer: There is no permanently free plan, but every paid plan includes a 30-day money-back guarantee.

faqs.question: What is SpyFu's Kombat tool
faqs.answer: Kombat is a keyword overlap comparison tool that visualizes shared and missed keywords across up to three competing domains.

faqs.question: Can SpyFu track my own keyword rankings
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include weekly rank tracking, with higher tiers tracking more keywords per week.

faqs.question: Does SpyFu show competitor PPC ads
faqs.answer: Yes, SpyFu shows historical Google Ads copy and bid keywords for any domain going back many years.

faqs.question: How is SpyFu different from Ahrefs or SEMrush
faqs.answer: SpyFu focuses specifically on competitor SEO and PPC intelligence at a lower price point, while Ahrefs and SEMrush offer broader technical SEO auditing suites.

faqs.question: Does SpyFu offer an agency plan
faqs.answer: Yes, the Team or Agency plan includes multiple user seats, white-label reporting, and API access.

faqs.question: Is SpyFu good for PPC research
faqs.answer: Yes, SpyFu is particularly strong for researching a competitor's historical paid search ad copy and bid keywords.

use_cases.title: Freelance SEO and PPC research
use_cases.description: Solo marketers use the Basic plan to research competitor keywords and ad copy for a single client or site.

use_cases.title: Content and keyword gap analysis
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use Kombat and RivalFlow to find keyword gaps versus direct competitors and prioritize content.

use_cases.title: Agency competitor reporting
use_cases.description: Agencies use the Team plan's multiple seats and white-label reports to deliver ongoing competitor intelligence to clients.

features: Unlimited search results, exports, and tracking projects

enrichment.short_description: SQD (formerly Subsquid) is an open-source onchain data platform that delivers validated, real-time and historical blockchain data across 225-plus networks through a single streaming API.
enrichment.seo_title: SQD (Subsquid) Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Use Cases
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SQD (formerly Subsquid) is an open-source blockchain data indexing platform. See features, pricing, and use cases for developers building on-chain apps.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SQD, formerly known as Subsquid, is an open-source blockchain data infrastructure platform founded in 2021 and headquartered in Zug, Switzerland under Subsquid Labs GmbH. It gives Web3 developers a single streaming API to access validated real-time and historical data from more than 225 blockchain networks, ingesting raw blocks, running cryptographic validation checks, and replicating data across a decentralized network of worker nodes. It is used by DeFi protocols, blockchain analytics platforms, gaming projects, and AI agents that need fast, trustworthy on-chain data, with reported customers including GMX, Morpho, and PancakeSwap collectively securing over 20 billion dollars in total value locked. The core Squid SDK and Pipes SDK are open source under an Apache 2.0 license, and the SQD Network and Portal components are open source under AGPL-3.0, allowing self-hosting. SQD Cloud offers a free playground for developing and deploying a single squid indexer at no cost, with production usage priced on a pay-as-you-go basis based on compute and storage consumption rather than raw data access, since network data retrieval itself is free. In October 2025, Subsquid Labs was acquired by Rezolve Ai, which announced a strategic repositioning toward enterprise and institutional data clients beyond Web3. SQD is best known for high-throughput, low-latency data delivery (median response times around 27 milliseconds) and a 99.9 percent uptime SLA for enterprise customers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SQD
paragraphs: SQD is an open-source, decentralized onchain data platform, formerly branded as Subsquid, that gives developers a single API for accessing validated blockchain data from over 225 networks.

Founded in 2021 in Zug, Switzerland, SQD was built to solve the reliability and cost problems of raw blockchain RPC access by validating every block through cryptographic checks and replicating data across a distributed network of worker nodes before serving it through a streaming HTTP API.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Developers query historical and real-time data from genesis to the current block head through the same API, with support for both EVM chains and Solana, and can self-host the network components since the core SDKs are open source.

The platform validates each block using multiple cryptographic checks and cross-references multiple data sources, and provides worker-signed responses with verifiable audit trails, which is why it is used by DeFi, gaming, and compliance-focused applications that require high data correctness.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The core Squid SDK, Pipes SDK, and CLI are free and open source, and SQD Network data retrieval itself is free of charge since the network is designed to make raw blockchain data widely accessible.

SQD Cloud offers a free playground for deploying a single indexer at no cost, while production deployments are billed pay-as-you-go based on the compute and database storage resources consumed, with enterprise plans adding a 99.9 percent uptime SLA.

features.title: Unified Streaming Data API
features.description: Access historical and real-time blockchain data from genesis to the latest block through one consistent HTTP API across 225-plus networks.

features.title: Cryptographic Data Validation
features.description: Every block passes multiple cryptographic checks and cross-source verification before being served, targeting 100 percent data correctness.

features.title: Decentralized Worker Network
features.description: Data is replicated across thousands of worker nodes that sign their responses, creating a verifiable audit trail.

features.title: Open-Source SDKs
features.description: The Squid SDK and Pipes SDK are Apache 2.0 licensed, letting developers build and self-host custom indexing pipelines.

features.title: Multi-Chain and Solana Support
features.description: A single API surface covers EVM-compatible chains as well as Solana, simplifying multi-chain application development.

features.title: Free Playground Environment
features.description: Every account gets a free playground organization to develop and deploy one squid indexer at no cost.

features.title: Low-Latency Delivery
features.description: Reports median response times around 27 milliseconds and sub-50 millisecond P90 latency under load.

features.title: Enterprise SLA and Self-Hosting
features.description: Enterprise customers get a 99.9 percent uptime SLA, and the AGPL-3.0 licensed network components can be self-hosted for full control.

pros: Core SDKs and data access are free and open source
Very low latency and high data correctness for on-chain queries
Single API covers 225-plus networks instead of separate integrations
Self-hosting option available for teams wanting full control
Used in production by well-known DeFi protocols managing billions in TVL
cons: Cloud pricing is usage-based and less predictable than flat subscription tools
Recent acquisition by Rezolve Ai introduces uncertainty about product direction
Primarily targeted at Web3 and blockchain developers, not a general-purpose tool
Requires technical setup and indexing knowledge to get full value
Company size and support structure post-acquisition are not publicly detailed

faqs.question: Is SQD free to use
faqs.answer: The core SDKs and CLI are free and open source, network data retrieval is free, and SQD Cloud includes a free playground for a single indexer; production cloud usage is billed pay-as-you-go.

faqs.question: What was SQD previously called
faqs.answer: SQD was previously known as Subsquid before rebranding.

faqs.question: How many blockchain networks does SQD support
faqs.answer: SQD supports data access across more than 225 blockchain networks through a single API.

faqs.question: Is SQD open source
faqs.answer: Yes, the Squid SDK and Pipes SDK are Apache 2.0 licensed, and the SQD Network and Portal are AGPL-3.0 licensed, allowing self-hosting.

faqs.question: Who owns SQD now
faqs.answer: Subsquid Labs, the company behind SQD, was acquired by Rezolve Ai in October 2025.

faqs.question: What is SQD used for
faqs.answer: SQD is used to index and query historical and real-time blockchain data for DeFi protocols, analytics platforms, gaming projects, and AI agents that need on-chain data.

faqs.question: Does SQD support Solana
faqs.answer: Yes, Solana is supported through the same API used for EVM-compatible chains.

faqs.question: How fast is SQD's data delivery
faqs.answer: SQD reports a median response time of around 27 milliseconds and roughly 48.5 milliseconds at the 90th percentile under load.

use_cases.title: DeFi protocol data indexing
use_cases.description: Protocols index on-chain events like swaps, deposits, and liquidations in real time to power dashboards and risk systems.
use_cases.audience: DeFi and blockchain protocol engineering teams

use_cases.title: Blockchain analytics platforms
use_cases.description: Analytics products query historical and real-time on-chain data across many networks through a single API instead of managing separate RPC integrations.
use_cases.audience: Web3 analytics and data platform builders

use_cases.title: AI agents needing on-chain data
use_cases.description: AI agents use validated, low-latency on-chain data feeds to make trustworthy decisions involving blockchain state.
use_cases.audience: AI and autonomous agent developers building on-chain

features: Free public Portal endpoint, no API key required
Priced by compute and database storage consumed

enrichment.short_description: SQLMesh is an open-source data transformation framework, backwards compatible with dbt, that gives data teams DevOps-style version control, testing, and safe deployment for SQL and Python data pipelines.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (open-source); Tobiko Cloud is custom priced
enrichment.seo_title: SQLMesh Review 2026: Features, Pricing vs dbt
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SQLMesh is a free, open-source dbt-compatible data transformation framework by Tobiko Data. See features, pricing, and how it compares to dbt.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SQLMesh is an open-source data transformation framework created by Tobiko Data, a company founded in 2022 by Iaroslav Zeigerman and brothers Tyson and Toby Mao, headquartered in San Mateo, California. It is designed for data engineers and analytics engineers who want DevOps-style practices, such as environment isolation, automated testing, and safe deployments, applied to SQL and Python data pipelines, and it is built to be format-compatible with dbt so existing dbt projects can be imported with minor changes. Its core differentiators include virtual environments that let developers spin up a full replica of a production environment without physically cloning data, intelligent incremental processing that only recomputes changed partitions instead of the whole DAG, compile-time SQL validation powered by the SQLGlot parser, and native blue-green deployment where promoting to production atomically swaps an environment pointer. The open-source project is free and licensed under Apache 2.0, with unlimited projects and no seat limits. Tobiko Cloud, the company's paid hosted offering, adds role-based access control, governance, single sign-on, and a more performant managed scheduler on top of the open-source core, priced through a platform fee plus pay-as-you-go consumption rather than per-seat licensing; exact pricing requires contacting Tobiko's sales team. SQLMesh is most often compared directly to dbt as a lower-overhead, cost-saving alternative for teams running large-scale SQL transformation pipelines.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SQLMesh
paragraphs: SQLMesh is an open-source data transformation framework that applies DevOps practices, such as environment isolation, automated testing, and safe deployments, to SQL and Python-based data pipelines.

It was created by Tobiko Data, founded in 2022 by data engineers from companies including Airbnb, Apple, and Netflix, and is designed to be format-compatible with dbt so teams can migrate existing dbt projects with only minor changes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SQLMesh's virtual environments let developers create a full replica of any environment instantly without physically duplicating data, which removes much of the storage and compute cost of traditional development environments.

Its incremental engine understands time-partitioned, idempotent, and forward-only models, recomputing only the partitions that actually changed, while the built-in SQLGlot parser catches SQL errors at compile time and native blue-green deployment ensures production never shows a half-built table.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The core SQLMesh framework is free and open source under an Apache 2.0 license, with no limit on projects, models, or contributors.

Tobiko Cloud, the company's managed offering, adds role-based access control, governance, SSO, and a hosted scheduler on top of the open-source features, priced through a platform fee combined with pay-as-you-go consumption; specific pricing is only available by contacting Tobiko's sales team.

features.title: Virtual Environments
features.description: Instantly spin up an isolated replica of any environment without physically cloning data, making development environments effectively free.

features.title: Intelligent Incremental Models
features.description: Understands time-partitioned, idempotent, and forward-only incremental models and only recomputes changed partitions.

features.title: dbt Compatibility
features.description: Format-compatible with dbt projects, allowing teams to import existing dbt pipelines with minor changes.

features.title: Compile-Time SQL Validation
features.description: Uses the SQLGlot parser to catch SQL errors before a pipeline runs, rather than failing mid-execution.

features.title: Blue-Green Deployments
features.description: Promotes changes to production by atomically swapping an environment pointer, so production never sees a partially built table.

features.title: Native Python Models
features.description: Python models are first-class citizens in the same DAG, with the same change-detection guarantees as SQL models.

features.title: Automated Testing and Data Quality Checks
features.description: Data quality checks run before a table is exposed to consumers, not after, catching issues earlier.

features.title: Tobiko Cloud Governance
features.description: The paid cloud offering adds role-based access control, SSO, and a managed scheduler for enterprise governance needs.

pros: Core framework is free, open source, and Apache 2.0 licensed
Meaningfully reduces compute and storage costs versus full DAG re-runs
dbt-compatible, easing migration from existing dbt projects
Compile-time SQL validation catches errors before they run in production
Built by experienced data engineers from major tech companies
cons: Smaller community and ecosystem than the more established dbt
Tobiko Cloud pricing is not publicly listed and requires a sales conversation
Small company size (2-10 employees at Tobiko Data) relative to larger data tooling vendors
Fewer third-party integrations and pre-built packages compared to dbt's package hub
Learning curve for teams unfamiliar with virtual environments and its plan-based workflow

faqs.question: Is SQLMesh free
faqs.answer: Yes, the core SQLMesh framework is free and open source under an Apache 2.0 license.

faqs.question: Is SQLMesh compatible with dbt
faqs.answer: Yes, SQLMesh is designed to be format-compatible with dbt, and existing dbt projects can typically be imported with minor changes.

faqs.question: Who created SQLMesh
faqs.answer: SQLMesh was created by Tobiko Data, founded in 2022 by Iaroslav Zeigerman, Tyson Mao, and Toby Mao.

faqs.question: What is Tobiko Cloud
faqs.answer: Tobiko Cloud is Tobiko Data's paid, hosted version of SQLMesh that adds role-based access control, SSO, governance, and a managed scheduler.

faqs.question: How much does Tobiko Cloud cost
faqs.answer: Tobiko Cloud pricing combines a platform fee with pay-as-you-go consumption; exact pricing requires contacting Tobiko's sales team.

faqs.question: What are SQLMesh's virtual environments
faqs.answer: Virtual environments let developers instantly create an isolated replica of any environment without physically duplicating the underlying data.

faqs.question: Does SQLMesh support Python models
faqs.answer: Yes, Python models are first-class and participate in the same dependency graph and change-detection system as SQL models.

faqs.question: What license is SQLMesh released under
faqs.answer: The open-source SQLMesh framework is released under the Apache 2.0 license.

use_cases.title: Migrating off dbt for cost savings
use_cases.description: Data teams move existing dbt projects to SQLMesh to cut compute and storage costs through smarter incremental processing and virtual environments.
use_cases.audience: Data engineering teams running large dbt pipelines

use_cases.title: DevOps-style data pipeline management
use_cases.description: Teams use blue-green deployments and compile-time validation to ship data model changes safely without breaking production tables.
use_cases.audience: Analytics engineers and data platform teams

use_cases.title: Enterprise governance with Tobiko Cloud
use_cases.description: Organizations use Tobiko Cloud's RBAC, SSO, and managed scheduler to run SQLMesh at scale with enterprise access controls.
use_cases.audience: Enterprise data platform and governance teams

enrichment.short_description: SquadCast (squadcast.fm) is a remote recording studio that lets podcasters and video creators capture separate, high-quality local audio and video tracks with guests from anywhere.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SquadCast.fm is a remote podcast and video recording studio. See its free plan, paid pricing, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SquadCast, found at squadcast.fm, is a remote recording studio for podcasters and video creators, co-founded in 2016 by Zachariah Moreno and Rockwell Felder and originally headquartered in Oakland, California. It lets hosts and guests record separate, studio-quality local audio and video tracks from anywhere with an internet connection, then automatically uploads and syncs those tracks in the cloud to avoid the compression and sync issues common with call-recording tools. The platform includes Dolby-powered audio processing, 4K video recording and export on higher tiers, built-in AI features for transcription, overdub, and content repurposing, and team collaboration tools for producers managing multiple shows. SquadCast was acquired by AI-powered editing platform Descript in August 2023 and now operates as SquadCast.fm by Descript; it is also bundled free with any Descript subscription. Pricing runs on a freemium model: a Free plan offers 1 recording hour per editor per month with a watermark; Hobbyist is 16 dollars per month billed annually (24 dollars monthly) with 10 hours and no watermark; Creator is 24 dollars per month billed annually (35 dollars monthly) with 30 hours, 4K exports, and unlimited AI features; and Business is 50 dollars per month billed annually (65 dollars monthly) with 40 hours, priority support, and unlimited AI regeneration and overdub for larger teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SquadCast
paragraphs: SquadCast is a browser-based remote recording studio built specifically for podcasters and video creators who need to record high-quality, separate local tracks with guests in different locations.

Co-founded in 2016 and originally based in Oakland, California, SquadCast was acquired by Descript in August 2023 and now operates as SquadCast.fm by Descript, with its technology increasingly integrated into the broader Descript editing platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Each participant's audio and video is recorded locally in the browser and uploaded to the cloud after the session, which avoids the choppy audio and lip-sync drift common with real-time call-recording software.

Higher tiers add Dolby audio processing, 4K video export, built-in AI tools for transcription and overdub, and team features like multiple shows, integrations, and priority support for producers managing recurring recording sessions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SquadCast uses a freemium model with a Free plan offering 1 recording hour per editor per month, and three paid tiers: Hobbyist at 16 dollars per month (annual billing), Creator at 24 dollars per month (annual billing), and Business at 50 dollars per month (annual billing), each roughly 40-50 percent higher when billed monthly.
Unused recording hours roll over up to twice the plan's monthly allotment, extra hours can be purchased a la carte, and the tool is included at no additional cost with any Descript subscription.

features.title: Separate Local Track Recording
features.description: Records each participant's audio and video locally in their browser for studio-quality tracks free of internet-call compression.

features.title: Automatic Cloud Sync and Backup
features.description: Tracks upload and sync automatically after the session, protecting against dropped connections mid-recording.

features.title: Dolby Audio Processing
features.description: Built-in Dolby.io audio enhancement cleans up recordings on paid plans.

features.title: 4K Video Recording and Export
features.description: Higher-tier plans support up to 4K video capture and export for video podcasts and YouTube content.

features.title: AI Transcription and Overdub
features.description: Automatic transcription plus AI voice regeneration and overdub tools for fixing flubbed lines without re-recording.

features.title: Multi-Show and Team Collaboration
features.description: Manage multiple shows and invite team members or co-producers with shared access on paid plans.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with third-party publishing and workflow tools to streamline the path from recording to a published episode.

features.title: Descript Bundle Access
features.description: Included free with any Descript subscription, tying recording directly into Descript's editing workflow.

pros: Free plan available with no credit card required to start
Local recording avoids the audio quality issues of live call-recording tools
Bundled free with a Descript subscription
Rollover recording hours reduce waste for infrequent podcasters
AI transcription and overdub built in rather than a separate purchase
cons: Now positioned primarily as an add-on to Descript rather than a fully independent product
Free plan recordings include a watermark and are limited to 1 hour per month
4K video and unlimited AI features require the pricier Creator or Business tier
Smaller standalone team since folding into Descript, which may affect long-term standalone development
Monthly billing carries a notable premium over annual billing

faqs.question: Is SquadCast free
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a Free plan offering 1 recording hour per editor per month with a watermark; paid plans start at 16 dollars per month billed annually.

faqs.question: Who owns SquadCast now
faqs.answer: SquadCast was acquired by Descript in August 2023 and now operates as SquadCast.fm by Descript.

faqs.question: Does SquadCast record separate tracks for each guest
faqs.answer: Yes, each participant's audio and video is recorded locally and separately, then synced in the cloud after the session.

faqs.question: Can I get SquadCast for free with Descript
faqs.answer: Yes, SquadCast is included at no additional cost with any Descript subscription.

faqs.question: Does SquadCast support 4K video
faqs.answer: 4K video recording and export is available on the Creator and Business plans.

faqs.question: What happens to unused recording hours on SquadCast
faqs.answer: Unused hours roll over automatically, up to twice the plan's monthly hour allotment.

faqs.question: Does SquadCast have AI features
faqs.answer: Yes, plans include AI transcription, and paid tiers add unlimited AI overdub and voice regeneration.

faqs.question: Who founded SquadCast
faqs.answer: SquadCast was co-founded in 2016 by Zachariah Moreno and Rockwell Felder.

use_cases.title: Solo and hobbyist podcasters
use_cases.description: Casual podcasters use the Free or Hobbyist plan to record interviews with remote guests without investing in studio equipment.

use_cases.title: Video podcast production
use_cases.description: Creators use 4K recording and Dolby audio processing to produce video podcasts for YouTube and social clips.

use_cases.title: Podcast networks and production teams
use_cases.description: Producers manage multiple shows, invite team members, and use AI overdub to speed up editing across a recurring show schedule.

features: 1 recording hour per editor per month
10 recording hours per editor per month
30 recording hours per editor per month
Unlimited basic and advanced AI suite
40 recording hours per editor per month

enrichment.short_description: Square is a payments and point-of-sale platform from Block, Inc. that gives businesses hardware, software, and processing to accept payments, manage inventory, and run their operations.
enrichment.seo_title: Square Review 2026: POS Pricing and Processing Fees
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Square pricing has a free plan plus Plus and Premium tiers from $49/month. Compare processing fees, features, pros and cons of Square POS.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Square is a payments and point-of-sale platform founded in 2009 by Jack Dorsey and Jim McKelvey, now operated by parent company Block, Inc., which is headquartered in Oakland, California and publicly traded on the NYSE under the ticker SQ. Square provides hardware readers and terminals, point-of-sale software, online checkout, invoicing, inventory management, payroll, and business banking tools aimed at small and mid-sized retailers, restaurants, and service businesses. Its pricing model is freemium and largely transaction-fee driven: the Free plan has no monthly subscription cost and charges 2.6 percent plus 15 cents per in-person transaction and 3.3 percent plus 30 cents per online transaction; Square Plus is 49 dollars per month per location and lowers in-person fees to 2.5 percent plus 15 cents and online fees to 2.9 percent plus 30 cents; and Square Premium is 149 dollars per month per location with the lowest fees at 2.4 percent plus 15 cents in-person. Manually keyed-in payments are charged 3.5 percent plus 15 cents across all plans. Businesses processing over 250,000 dollars per year can request custom pricing. Square is one of the most recognizable names in small business payments, competing with Toast, Clover, and Stripe Terminal, and its parent Block, Inc. also owns Cash App, Afterpay, and Tidal.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Square
paragraphs: Square is a payments and point-of-sale platform that gives businesses the hardware and software to accept card payments in person and online, alongside tools for inventory, invoicing, payroll, and banking.

Founded in 2009 by Jack Dorsey and Jim McKelvey, Square's parent company rebranded to Block, Inc. in 2021 to reflect its expansion into Cash App, Afterpay, and other fintech products, though the Square brand and product line continue to operate under the Block umbrella from its Oakland, California headquarters.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: At its core, Square offers point-of-sale software paired with card readers and terminals for retail, restaurant, and service businesses, plus a free online store and checkout for e-commerce sales.

Beyond payments, Square offers inventory tracking, digital invoicing, appointment booking, payroll, team management, and Square Banking tools like business loans and a business debit card, all accessible from the same account.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Square uses a freemium, transaction-fee-based pricing model: the Free plan has no monthly fee and charges 2.6 percent plus 15 cents per in-person transaction, while Square Plus (49 dollars per month per location) and Square Premium (149 dollars per month per location) lower per-transaction processing rates in exchange for the subscription fee.

Online transactions are charged 3.3 percent plus 30 cents on the Free plan, dropping to 2.9 percent plus 30 cents on Plus and Premium, and manually keyed payments are a flat 3.5 percent plus 15 cents across every plan. Businesses processing over 250,000 dollars annually can negotiate custom processing rates.

features.title: Point-of-Sale Software and Hardware
features.description: Card readers, terminals, and registers paired with POS software for retail, restaurant, and service businesses.

features.title: Online Store and Checkout
features.description: A free online store builder with integrated checkout that syncs inventory with in-person sales.

features.title: Inventory Management
features.description: Track stock levels, get low-stock alerts, and sync inventory across online and in-person sales channels.

features.title: Invoicing and Payment Links
features.description: Send digital invoices and shareable payment links for remote or service-based transactions.

features.title: Payroll and Team Management
features.description: Run payroll, track employee hours, and manage permissions across staff accounts.

features.title: Square Banking
features.description: Business banking tools including a business debit card, savings, and access to Square Loans.

features.title: Appointments and Scheduling
features.description: Booking tools for service businesses to manage client appointments and reminders.

features.title: Analytics and Reporting
features.description: Built-in sales reporting and dashboards to track revenue, trends, and staff performance.

pros: Free plan with no monthly subscription fee to get started
Transparent, publicly listed processing rates with no long-term contract
Wide ecosystem covering payments, payroll, banking, and inventory in one account
Backed by a large public company (Block, Inc.) with continuous product investment
Custom pricing available for high-volume merchants
cons: Per-transaction processing fees can be costly at high sales volume compared to negotiated merchant accounts
Monthly subscription required to unlock the lowest processing rates
Manually keyed payments carry the highest fee across all plans
Block, Inc. underwent significant layoffs in 2026, which may affect support resourcing
Some advanced features are gated behind Plus or Premium plans

faqs.question: Is Square free to use
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan has no monthly subscription cost; you only pay processing fees per transaction.

faqs.question: What are Square's processing fees
faqs.answer: In-person transactions are 2.6 percent plus 15 cents on the Free plan, dropping to 2.4 percent plus 15 cents on Premium; online transactions range from 3.3 percent plus 30 cents down to 2.9 percent plus 30 cents depending on plan.

faqs.question: Who owns Square
faqs.answer: Square is a product line of Block, Inc., the publicly traded fintech company (NYSE: SQ) headquartered in Oakland, California.

faqs.question: How much does Square Plus cost
faqs.answer: Square Plus costs 49 dollars per month per location and lowers per-transaction processing rates compared to the Free plan.

faqs.question: Does Square offer custom pricing
faqs.answer: Yes, businesses processing over 250,000 dollars per year can contact Square's sales team for custom processing rates.

faqs.question: Can Square be used for online sales
faqs.answer: Yes, Square includes a free online store and checkout that syncs inventory with in-person sales.

faqs.question: Does Square require a long-term contract
faqs.answer: No, Square has no locked-in contracts and merchants can cancel or switch plans at any time.

faqs.question: When was Square founded
faqs.answer: Square was founded in 2009 by Jack Dorsey and Jim McKelvey.

use_cases.title: Retail point-of-sale
use_cases.description: Small and mid-sized retailers use Square hardware and software to ring up sales, track inventory, and sync online and in-store stock.

use_cases.title: Restaurant and food service payments
use_cases.description: Restaurants and cafes use Square terminals and POS software to process in-person orders and manage staff and tips.
use_cases.audience: Restaurants, cafes, and food service businesses

use_cases.title: Service business invoicing and booking
use_cases.description: Service providers use Square Invoices and Appointments to bill clients and manage scheduling without in-person hardware.

features: For businesses processing over $250,000 per year

enrichment.short_description: Squarespace is an all-in-one website builder that combines designer templates, hosting, e-commerce, and email marketing tools for individuals and small businesses to build and run a site.
enrichment.headquarters: New York City, New York, USA
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Squarespace pricing starts at $16/month. Compare Basic, Core, Plus, and Advanced plans, features, pros and cons of this website builder.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Squarespace is an all-in-one website builder founded in 2003 by Anthony Casalena and headquartered in New York City, where it occupies a large office in Manhattan's West Village. It is designed for individuals, creators, and small to mid-sized businesses that want to build a professional website, online store, or portfolio without writing code, offering designer templates, a drag-and-drop editor, managed hosting, built-in SEO tools, and AI-assisted site building. Squarespace has grown to roughly 2,000 employees and trades similarly named but is entirely unrelated to the payments company Square. Pricing is subscription-based across four annually billed tiers: Basic at 16 dollars per month with a 2 percent commerce fee, Core at 23 dollars per month which waives commerce transaction fees and unlocks advanced sales tools, Plus at 39 dollars per month with lower payment processing rates for growing sellers, and Advanced at 99 dollars per month with the lowest processing rates and advanced developer and enterprise features. Every plan includes hosting, SSL security, mobile-optimized templates, SEO tools, and 24/7 customer support, and a 14-day free trial is available, though a paid plan is required to publish a site. Annual subscriptions include one year of free domain registration. Squarespace also offers built-in email marketing campaigns, member areas, and digital product sales, positioning it as a direct competitor to Wix, Webflow, and Shopify for site-building and light e-commerce use cases.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Squarespace
paragraphs: Squarespace is an all-in-one website builder that lets individuals and businesses design, host, and manage a website, online store, or portfolio without writing code.

Founded in 2003 by Anthony Casalena and headquartered in New York City, Squarespace has grown from a one-person blog hosting service into a major website builder with a large template library, built-in e-commerce, and AI-assisted design tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform's core is a drag-and-drop editor paired with professionally designed templates that adapt automatically to mobile devices, plus built-in SEO tools, managed hosting, and SSL security on every plan.

Higher tiers add e-commerce features like unlimited products with variants, lower or waived transaction fees, member areas and digital content sales, and built-in email marketing campaigns with segmentation and automation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Squarespace offers four annually billed plans: Basic at 16 dollars per month, Core at 23 dollars per month, Plus at 39 dollars per month, and Advanced at 99 dollars per month, with monthly billing available at a higher rate.

Commerce fees decrease as plans go up, from a 2 percent transaction fee on Basic down to 0 percent on higher tiers, and annual plans include one year of free domain registration along with a 14-day free trial to explore the editor before publishing.

features.title: Drag-and-Drop Website Builder
features.description: Professionally designed templates combined with a visual editor for customizing layout, colors, and fonts without code.

features.title: Built-In E-Commerce
features.description: Sell physical products, digital downloads, memberships, and courses with inventory and variant management.

features.title: Email Marketing Campaigns
features.description: Design, schedule, and automate email campaigns using the same brand assets as the website.

features.title: SEO Tools
features.description: Built-in SEO settings, clean URL structures, and automatic sitemap generation to help pages get indexed.

features.title: AI Website Builder
features.description: AI-assisted tools help generate initial site layouts, copy suggestions, and design choices during setup.

features.title: Squarespace Payments
features.description: Native payment processing supporting major cards and digital wallets with tiered processing rates by plan.

features.title: Member Areas and Digital Content
features.description: Gate content, courses, and community areas behind paid or free membership tiers.

features.title: Managed Hosting and Security
features.description: Unlimited bandwidth, SSL certificates, and cloud hosting included on every plan with no separate hosting purchase needed.

pros: Polished, designer-quality templates out of the box
All-in-one pricing includes hosting, SSL, and support with no add-on hosting fees
Built-in email marketing and e-commerce reduce the need for separate tools
Transaction fees decrease or disappear on higher-tier plans
14-day free trial to test the editor before committing
cons: A paid plan is required to publish a site; the free trial cannot go live
Less design and code flexibility than developer-first builders like Webflow
E-commerce transaction fees still apply on the entry-level Basic plan
Advanced e-commerce and developer features require the priciest Advanced plan
Switching templates after launch can require substantial rebuilding

faqs.question: How much does Squarespace cost
faqs.answer: Plans start at 16 dollars per month for Basic (billed annually) and go up to 99 dollars per month for the Advanced plan.

faqs.question: Does Squarespace have a free plan
faqs.answer: No, but there is a 14-day free trial; a paid plan is required to publish a live site.

faqs.question: Which Squarespace plan removes transaction fees
faqs.answer: The Core plan and above waive Squarespace's commerce transaction fees, while the Basic plan still charges a 2 percent fee on sales.

faqs.question: Is Squarespace good for e-commerce
faqs.answer: Yes, Squarespace supports physical products, digital downloads, memberships, and courses, with lower payment processing rates on the Plus and Advanced plans.

faqs.question: Is Squarespace the same company as Square
faqs.answer: No, Squarespace (website builder) and Square (payments and POS, part of Block, Inc.) are two completely unrelated companies despite the similar names.

faqs.question: Does Squarespace include email marketing
faqs.answer: Yes, built-in email marketing campaigns with templates, scheduling, and segmentation are included on Squarespace plans.

faqs.question: Who founded Squarespace
faqs.answer: Squarespace was founded in 2003 by Anthony Casalena while he was a student at the University of Maryland.

faqs.question: Does Squarespace offer a free domain
faqs.answer: Yes, annual subscriptions include one year of free domain registration.

use_cases.title: Portfolio and personal brand websites
use_cases.description: Creators, photographers, and freelancers use designer templates to build a polished personal site without hiring a developer.

use_cases.title: Small business online stores
use_cases.description: Small retailers use built-in e-commerce, inventory, and payment tools to sell products directly from their website.
use_cases.audience: Small business owners and independent sellers

use_cases.title: Membership and digital content sites
use_cases.description: Coaches and educators use member areas and digital product sales to distribute paid courses and gated content.
use_cases.audience: Coaches, educators, and digital content creators
